author: @frankbenevolence
text: Even in a selfish view of the world I don't think it was at all foolish.  If you had refused to help, he would still be unlikely to pay you.  His gain doesn't cost you anything and helping people and feeling useful feels good.  I call that a win, you get both happiness and reputational gain and lose basically nothing.

author: @benjamindemontgomery6317
text: If you look after yourself there is only one person locking after you. if you look out for others you can comfortably look after 5 to 10 people and suddenly there are 10 people looking after you. and as a result you are safer and you don't have to worry so much  anymore. a great example of this is countries with a social safety net like the Scandinavian countries. but doing this on a personal level is even more powerful. I started up a MENZSHED were i live there are about 20 of us that come together every week and do projects for the community. Your streams Robert are very inspirational for me I bring that along to my friends. I appreciate what you do.

author: @marcfruchtman9473
text: Thank you for doing what you do.

author: @elarcadenoah9000
text: you are like nicolas tesla a person that is a good samaritan for the world and i think all of us should be the same to be happy share to improve others life,the more u do for others god will give u more in the other life our mission in this world is to aproach the heaven in to the earth with good actions and with praying too but booth are more conected than we think

author: @grendel1960a
text: Many years back I acquired  access to a skip full of old broken telescopes, after asking permission  I collected some of these, they were all broken, but contained useful parts, on a worldwide telescope forum, I saw many people who needed some of those parts, so I would get in touch, and send them the parts - at my expense. Many would ask to pay, my response was to pay it forward, do something  for someone else, donate to charity, or whatever. The idea was that by paying it forward, more people benefit from that single gift, and happiness is  spread  throughout the world.

text:
Well said sir. It has to start somewhere.
I remember being viewed with suspicion when I freely helped.
Then there is the Russian adage which goes if you help a struggling man he will never stand on his own feet.
The answer depends on the need and the merit.

author: @OMIF1
text: Little things, big impacts. We are taught all wrong! My better half was cleaning the fridge and wound up tossing about half a pound of food. I said, that is wasteful. She said "It's only a little bit". I looked up some numbers: There are about 150 million households in the USA. If everyone tosses a "little bit" of food, like we do, it amounts to 75 million pounds of food wasted a week from just this one behavior. Yesterday, I used a paper napkin with lunch and I thought: 150 million paper napkins a day at lunch. 7 days in a week. That is 1,050,000,000 paper napkins a week. A little thing, a big cost in waste. So, yeah, no such thing as a "little thing". Have a great day.

replies.author: @OMIF1
replies.text: By the way, my nick name means "Open mouth, Insert foot" It's the name of a song I wrote.
